creator: Sunrychen@gmail.com
text:
On Wed, Oct 12, 2011 at 9:34 AM, wen heping <wenheping@gmail.com> wrote:

> prefixes: LOCALBASE=usr/JJJ PREFIX=/usr/JJJ
>
I can't redo it with my environment, we have different prefixes. Maybe this
is the reason caused the problem. Or did you chroot or something else?


> X11BASE=/usr/JJJ
> DISTDIR=/tmp/distfiles
> WRKDIRPREFIX=/work
> DISTCACHE=/distcache
> LOCALBASE=/usr/JJJ
> PWD=/usr/ports/chinese/cxterm
> making all in ./fonts...
> /usr/JJJ/bin/bdftopcf -t hku16et.bdf | gzip > hku16et.pcf.gz
>
The file has been made ok.


> installing in ./fonts...
> + mkdir -p /fonts/X11/chinese
> + /usr/bin/install -c -m 0444 hku16et.pcf.gz /fonts/X11/chinese
>
But been installed to /fonts/X11/chinese?
In my box it goes to /usr/local/lib/X11/fonts/chinese.


> ====================<phase 7: make package>====================
> ===>  Building package for zh-cxterm-5.0.3_3
> tar: lib/X11/fonts/chinese/hku16et.pcf.gz: Cannot stat: No such file
> or directory
>
Tar try to find the file in lib/X11/fonts/chinese, but it
at /fonts/X11/chinese in your environment.
